# Responsive Patterns

**Generated by**: design-nexus v3.0.0-enhanced  
**Purpose**: Document breakpoint usage and mobile-first responsive patterns  
**Scope**: Single component validation (Button) — full system rules would include all components

---

## Breakpoint Strategy

### Mobile-First Approach

This design system follows **mobile-first** responsive design:
- Start with mobile layout (< 640px)
- Progressively enhance for larger screens
- Use `min-width` media queries (not `max-width`)

**Why mobile-first?**
- Mobile usage dominates (60-70% of traffic)
- Forces content prioritization
- Easier to enhance than strip down
- Better performance on mobile devices

---

## Breakpoint Definitions

**⚠️ Note**: This design system does not define breakpoints in Figma. Recommended breakpoints below follow Tailwind CSS / shadcn/ui conventions.

| Breakpoint | Min Width | Target Devices | Use Case |
|---|---|---|---|
| `sm` | 640px | Large phones, small tablets | Single-column → multi-column |
| `md` | 768px | Tablets (portrait) | Expand spacing, larger text |
| `lg` | 1024px | Tablets (landscape), small laptops | Sidebars, complex layouts |
| `xl` | 1280px | Desktops | Maximum content width, multi-panel |
| `2xl` | 1536px | Large desktops | Extra-wide layouts (optional) |

**Most common usage**: `sm` breakpoint only (640px) — two-state responsive (mobile vs. desktop).

---

## Button Component Responsive Patterns

### Mobile (< 640px)

**Full-width buttons** for primary actions:
```css
.btn--mobile-full {
  width: 100%;
}

@media (min-width: 640px) {
  .btn--mobile-full {
    width: auto; /* Restore intrinsic width on desktop */
  }
}
```

**Stacked button groups**:
```html
<!-- Mobile: Vertical stack, full-width -->
<!-- Desktop: Horizontal row, auto-width -->
<div class="button-group">
  <button class="btn btn--primary">Submit</button>
  <button class="btn btn--outline">Cancel</button>
</div>
```

```css
.button-group {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: var(--spacing-sm);
}

.button-group .btn {
  width: 100%;
}

@media (min-width: 640px) {
  .button-group {
    flex-direction: row;
    gap: var(--spacing-md);
  }
  
  .button-group .btn {
    width: auto;
  }
}
```

### Touch Target Optimization

**⚠️ CRITICAL**: All interactive elements must meet **44×44px minimum touch target** (WCAG 2.2 AA).

**Current Button violations:**
- All variants: height=40px (need 44px minimum)
- Icon-only variants: 32×32px and 40×40px (need 44×44px)

**Fixed mobile touch targets**:
```css
.btn {
  min-height: 44px;
  min-width: 44px;
  padding: 8px 16px; /* Adjusted to maintain 44px height */
}

.btn--icon {
  width: 44px;
  height: 44px;
  padding: 12px; /* Centers 20×20px icon in 44×44px container */
}
```

---

## Responsive Typography

### Font Scaling

**Mobile-first scale** (base size increases at breakpoints):

```css
/* Mobile: Smaller base sizes */
.btn {
  font-size: var(--text-sm); /* 14px */
}

/* Desktop: Larger base sizes */
@media (min-width: 640px) {
  .btn {
    font-size: var(--text-base); /* 16px */
  }
}
```

**Button size variants** (already defined in component):
- `sm`: 14px (mobile) → 14px (desktop) — stays small
- `default`: 14px (mobile) → 16px (desktop) — scales up
- `lg`: 16px (mobile) → 18px (desktop) — scales up

---

## Layout Patterns

### Single-Column → Multi-Column

**Pattern**: Mobile stacks vertically, desktop displays as grid

**Example: Card grid with buttons**
```html
<div class="card-grid">
  <div class="card">
    <h3>Card Title</h3>
    <p>Content...</p>
    <button class="btn btn--primary">Action</button>
  </div>
  <!-- More cards... -->
</div>
```

```css
/* Mobile: Single column */
.card-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  gap: var(--spacing-md);
}

/* Tablet: 2 columns */
@media (min-width: 640px) {
  .card-grid {
    grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr);
    gap: var(--spacing-lg);
  }
}

/* Desktop: 3 columns */
@media (min-width: 1024px) {
  .card-grid {
    grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
  }
}
```

### Sticky Actions (Mobile)

**Pattern**: Action buttons stick to bottom on mobile, inline on desktop

```html
<div class="form-container">
  <form class="form">
    <!-- Form fields... -->
  </form>
  <div class="form-actions">
    <button class="btn btn--outline">Cancel</button>
    <button class="btn btn--primary">Submit</button>
  </div>
</div>
```

```css
/* Mobile: Sticky footer */
.form-container {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 100vh;
}

.form {
  flex: 1;
  padding: var(--spacing-md);
}

.form-actions {
  position: sticky;
  bottom: 0;
  display: flex;
  gap: var(--spacing-md);
  padding: var(--spacing-md);
  background-color: var(--color-surface);
  border-top: 1px solid var(--color-border);
  box-shadow: 0 -2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
}

.form-actions .btn {
  flex: 1; /* Full-width buttons on mobile */
}

/* Desktop: Inline actions, right-aligned */
@media (min-width: 640px) {
  .form-container {
    min-height: auto;
  }
  
  .form-actions {
    position: static;
    justify-content: flex-end;
    box-shadow: none;
    border-top: none;
  }
  
  .form-actions .btn {
    flex: 0; /* Auto-width buttons on desktop */
  }
}
```

---

## Icon Button Responsiveness

### Hidden Text on Mobile

**Pattern**: Show text label on desktop, icon-only on mobile

```html
<button class="btn btn--icon-responsive">
  <svg class="btn__icon" aria-hidden="true"><!-- icon --></svg>
  <span class="btn__text">Download</span>
</button>
```

```css
/* Mobile: Hide text, show icon only */
.btn--icon-responsive {
  padding: 12px;
  min-width: 44px;
  min-height: 44px;
}

.btn--icon-responsive .btn__text {
  display: none;
}

/* Desktop: Show text + icon */
@media (min-width: 640px) {
  .btn--icon-responsive {
    padding: 8px 16px;
  }
  
  .btn--icon-responsive .btn__text {
    display: inline;
  }
}
```

**⚠️ Accessibility**: Always include `aria-label` when hiding text:
```html
<button class="btn btn--icon-responsive" aria-label="Download Report">
  <svg><!-- icon --></svg>
  <span class="btn__text">Download</span>
</button>
```

---

## Dialog/Modal Responsiveness

### Full-Screen on Mobile

**Pattern**: Modals are full-screen on mobile, centered on desktop

```css
/* Mobile: Full-screen modal */
.dialog {
  position: fixed;
  inset: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 0;
  padding: var(--spacing-md);
  overflow-y: auto;
}

/* Desktop: Centered modal with max-width */
@media (min-width: 640px) {
  .dialog {
    position: fixed;
    inset: 50% auto auto 50%;
    transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
    width: 90%;
    max-width: 32rem; /* 512px */
    height: auto;
    max-height: 90vh;
    border-radius: var(--radius-xl);
    padding: var(--spacing-lg);
  }
}
```

### Button Layout in Modals

```css
/* Mobile: Stacked buttons, full-width */
.dialog__footer {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: var(--spacing-sm);
}

.dialog__footer .btn {
  width: 100%;
}

/* Desktop: Horizontal buttons, right-aligned */
@media (min-width: 640px) {
  .dialog__footer {
    flex-direction: row;
    justify-content: flex-end;
  }
  
  .dialog__footer .btn {
    width: auto;
  }
}
```

---

## Spacing Adjustments

### Padding/Margin Scaling

**Pattern**: Tighter spacing on mobile, more generous on desktop

```css
/* Mobile: Compact spacing */
.section {
  padding: var(--spacing-md) var(--spacing-md);
  gap: var(--spacing-md);
}

/* Desktop: Expanded spacing */
@media (min-width: 640px) {
  .section {
    padding: var(--spacing-lg) var(--spacing-xl);
    gap: var(--spacing-lg);
  }
}
```

---

## Performance Considerations

### Mobile-Specific Optimizations

**1. Reduce animation complexity on mobile:**
```css
@media (max-width: 639px) {
  .btn {
    transition: background-color 150ms ease; /* Simpler transition */
  }
}

@media (min-width: 640px) {
  .btn {
    transition: background-color 200ms ease, transform 150ms ease; /* Richer transition */
  }
}
```

**2. Respect user's motion preferences:**
```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  *,
  *::before,
  *::after {
    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
  }
}
```

---

## Testing Checklist

When implementing responsive patterns, test:

- [ ] **Mobile portrait** (375×667px) — iPhone SE, small phones
- [ ] **Mobile landscape** (667×375px) — Rotated phone
- [ ] **Tablet portrait** (768×1024px) — iPad, Android tablets
- [ ] **Tablet landscape** (1024×768px) — Rotated tablet
- [ ] **Desktop** (1280×720px) — Standard laptop
- [ ] **Large desktop** (1920×1080px) — External monitor

**Touch testing**:
- [ ] All interactive elements ≥ 44×44px
- [ ] Buttons have adequate spacing (no accidental taps)
- [ ] Sticky footers don't obscure content
- [ ] Modals are accessible on all screen sizes

**Accessibility testing**:
- [ ] Screen reader announces button labels correctly
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works on all screen sizes
- [ ] Focus indicators visible at all breakpoints
- [ ] Zoom to 200% doesn't break layout (WCAG 2.2 AA)

---

## Recommendations for Full System

This pattern file was generated from a **single component validation** (Button). For a complete design system, add:

1. **Breakpoint tokens** — Define breakpoints as CSS custom properties or JS constants
2. **Container queries** — Use `@container` for component-level responsiveness (when browser support allows)
3. **Responsive typography scale** — Full type ramp with mobile/desktop variants
4. **Form field responsive patterns** — Label placement, field widths, validation messages
5. **Navigation responsive patterns** — Mobile menu (hamburger), desktop navbar
6. **Table responsive patterns** — Horizontal scroll, card layout, collapsible rows
7. **Image responsive patterns** — `srcset`, `sizes`, lazy loading strategies

---
